CommunityApplications.Profile:
**UmlautAdaptarrEX**
UmlautAdaptarrEX is a title-rewriting proxy that sits between your *Arr stack (Sonarr, Radarr, Lidarr, Readarr, Prowlarr / NZB Hydra) and your indexers. It rewrites searches and Newznab/Torznab responses on the fly so releases with umlauts, German titles, TVDB aliases or non-English naming are reliably matched, downloaded and imported - releases that would otherwise be silently missed.
It is a full Next.js + Fastify + Prisma + SQLite rewrite of the legacy .NET UmlautAdaptarr.
**What it solves** - *Arrs miss releases when umlauts are written as `oe`/`ae`/`ue` instead of `Ă¶`/`Ă¤`/`ĂĽ` (or vice versa). - Sonarr/Radarr expect English titles from TheTVDB/TMDB - German productions break with "release matched to series by ID" errors. - Badly named releases (e.g. missing GERMAN tag) can optionally be renamed so the *Arrs detect them properly.
**Language plugins** Plugins are the core extensibility mechanism - they can be combined per library and define how titles are normalized for that language. Three plugins are shipped out of the box: - **German umlauts** (default) - `Ă¶ â†” oe`, `Ă¤ â†” ae`, `ĂĽ â†” ue`, `Ăź â†” ss` plus German title matching via TVDB/TMDB aliases - **Swedish umlauts** - `ĂĄ â†” a`, `Ă¤ â†” a`, `Ă¶ â†” o` for Swedish releases - **French accents** - `Ă© Ă¨ ĂŞ Ă« â†” e`, `Ă Ă˘ â†” a`, `Ă§ â†” c`, `ĂŻ Ă® â†” i`, `Ă´ â†” o`, `Ăą Ă» â†” u` for French releases
**What it can do** - Native support for Sonarr, Radarr (via alternateTitles + optional TMDB), Lidarr, Readarr, Prowlarr and NZB Hydra (Newznab + Torznab) - Multiple instances per *Arr type (e.g. two Sonarrs) - Configurable title providers in custom order: TVDB, TMDB, internal db-cache - Two operating modes: Prowlarr HTTP-CONNECT indexer proxy (port 5006) or direct-as-indexer (port 5005) - Built-in Web UI (port 5007) with setup wizard, dashboard, instance manager, sync runs and full request/rename history - Live log streaming via WebSocket directly in the browser - Persistent SQLite database under `/data` - no cache loss on restart, no external DB required - Bilingual UI: German, French, Swedish and English
**Links** - Source: https://github.com/xpsony/UmlautAdaptarrEX - Docker image: `lexfi/umlautadaptarrex:latest` - Support: open an issue on GitHub or join the Discord linked below.
